did.unittitle: Stanley B. Burns, M.D., historic medical photography collection

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: The collection includes photographic plates and prints collected by Stanley B. Burns, M.D., that document medical practice and medical practitioners circa 1840 through the 1970s. The collection includes items created using multiple photographic techniques and supports including daguerreotypes, ambrotypes, tintypes, lantern slides, photographic negatives, cartes de visite, cabinet photographs (cabinet cards), stereoscopic photographs, panoramic photographs, picture postcards, gelatin silver prints, albumen prints, and photolithographs. Most of the collection is organized by topic and/or format, with the exception of a group of framed photographs (the Archive Wall) formerly displayed at the Burns Archive. Approximately half of the photographic prints in the collections are contained in photograph albums. Materials include extensive holdings documenting physicians and medical scientists at work, operative scenes, hospital wards, laboratories, nurses and nursing, notable physicians, surgical specialties, war medicine, patients, and disease states. Published works in the collection have been individually cataloged.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Stanley B. Burns, M.D., 2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in 8 series: Series I. Archive wall. Series II. Hard images. Series III. Lantern slides. Series IV. Panoramic photographs. Series V. Assembled collections. Series VI. Special collections. Series VII. Medical photograph albums. Series VIII. Albums de l'internat.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Stanley B. Burns, M.D., is an ophthalmologist and Research Professor of Medicine and Psychiatry, Professor of Medical Humanities, and Adjunct Professor of Obstetrics and Gynecology at New York University Langone Medical Center and Medical School. He is also an author, curator, historian, collector, archivist, and television and film consultant.

In 1975 Dr. Burns began collecting unique historic photography documenting multiple subjects including medicine, war, death, crime, racism, Judaica, and African American history. In 1977, Dr. Burns founded the Burns Archive in his home located in the Brooklyn borough of New York City, to provide access to his collection of historic photography. The Burns Archive holdings currently contain over one million photographs.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Stanley B. Burns, M.D. Historic Medical Photography Collection. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection includes photographs collected by Stanley B. Burns, M.D., that document the history of medical science and medical practice circa 1840 through the 1970s. Items in the collection were created using multiple photographic techniques and supports including daguerreotypes, ambrotypes, tintypes, lantern slides, photographic negatives, cartes de visite, cabinet photographs (cabinet cards), stereoscopic photographs, panoramic photographs, picture postcards, gelatin silver prints, albumen prints, and photolithographs.

Materials in the collection are grouped in series and subseries by topic and/or format according to the order in which they were received from Dr. Burns. Description of materials in the collection, including subject terms and item numbers, is based on description provided by Dr. Burns when the collection was acquired.
Series I. Archive Wall includes 61 framed photographs which were formerly displayed at the Burns Archive. Photographic prints in the series cover a range of subjects and photographic techniques.

Series II. Hard images includes daguerreotypes, ambrotypes, and tintypes. Most items in this series are housed in original hinged cases. The series is divided in two subseries: Physicians (78 items) and Patients (151 items). The Physicians subseries includes images of physicians, medical practitioners, dentists, and medical students, often posed with skulls, medical equipment, or other accoutrements of their profession. The Patients subseries includes images of people with visible symptoms from a variety of medical conditions, some depict treatments.

Series III. Lantern slides includes 264 glass plate slides from 14 subseries. Slides include photographs of patients, medical personnel, medical equipment, and treatments; X-rays; tissue samples; diagrams; and illustrations. Most slides in the series appear to have been created for use in medical instruction. Slides in the Dr. William McFee subseries include color photography; most items in other subseries consist of black and white images.
Series IV. Panoramic photographs includes two large photographic prints.

Series V. Assembled collections includes photographic prints created using a variety of photographic techniques grouped into 33 subseries. Items in subseries were not acquired as established collections, but were grouped by Dr. Burns according to topic and/or format.

Series VI. Special collections incudes photographic prints created using a variety of photographic techniques grouped into 58 subseries. Items in subseries were acquired by Dr. Burns as established collections; many were created by a single medical practitioner or to document a project or event.

Series VII. Photograph albums includes 49 albums containing prints created using a variety of photographic techniques. Albums in the series were created by medical practitioners, organizations, or institutions. Albums document the personal lives of medical practitioners, medical institutions, and medical colleges.
Series VIII. Albums de l'Internat includes 13 annual volumes documenting interns at all of Paris' hospitals.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Published works from the Stanley B. Burns, M.D., Historic Medical Photography Collection have been individually cataloged and can be discovered by searching the library catalog.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Item numbers used by Dr. Burns have been retained in this series.

odd.head: General
odd.p: This series is unprocessed. Contact the Medical Historical Library at historical.library@yale.edu to request materials that can not be requested using Archives at Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formats in this series include ambrotypes, daguerreotypes, and tintypes (ferrotypes) in a variety of sizes. Most items are enclosed in cases, some items are framed. Item numbers have been retained from Burns' documentaion and are not always consecutive.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The sub-series includes images of physicians, surgeons, medical students, dentists, pharmacists, and homeopaths.

did.unittitle: 1. Early ether operation, Massachusetts General Hospital

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Framed 1/6 plate daguerreotype, possibly photographed by Albert Southworth and Josiah Hawes. Surgical scene in Massachussetts General Hospital operating theater, now known as the "Ether Dome". Glass covered case of surgical instruments is visible behind surgeons. Operating chair used for first surgery with administered ether is visible on the left. This appears to be a copy image of a staged operation scene commemorating the first ether operations.

did.unittitle: 4. James Samuel How, M.D. dead from cholera epidemic, St. Louis, Missouri

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in case with newspaper clipping pinned to inside cover. 3 1/4 x 3 1/2 x 5/8 inches. Post mortem portrait of young man resting on pillow. Obituary notice clipping states he was 22 years old, had "recently completed his studies at the Medical College of the State University (Missouri)," and died of Cholera after treating patients during an outbreak. Brown case with rose motif, red velvet lining, oval frame, hook clasp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/4 plate daguerreotype in a case. 3 1/4 x 4 5/8 x 5/8 inches. Portrait of seated physician with open tool kit on table. Brown case with gilt trim, red velvet lining, square inner frame, and two hook clasps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/4 plate ambrotype in a case. 3 3/4 x 4 3/4 x 3/4 inches. Photograph of a kneeling physician examining the ear of a young child sitting in a wooden chair. Woman sitting next to child is holding child still with one hand. Brown case with red velvet lining, arched inner frame, and two hook clasps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in a case. 3 1/4 x 3 3/4 x 5/8 inches. Portrait of a young man in patterned vest and light colored jacket holding tooth extractor in left hand. Brown case with floral motif, lined in red velvet, square frame, 2 hook clasps. Small sticker on top exterior edge says "dentist."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in a case. 3 3/8 x 3 3/4 x 3/4 inches. Man standing behind seated patient holding tooth extractor with one hand and opening patient's mouth with other hand. Dark brown case, brown velvet lining, oval frame, push button clasp. 2 small stickers on exterior "tooth extractor."

did.unittitle: 15. London surgeon Thomas M. Greenhow teaching innovative lithotomy technique

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate ambrotype in a case. Two men seated at table, man on left is holding surgical tool while man on right observes. Hand painted white highlights. Plain brown case with red velvet lining, arched interior frame, and 2 hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 16. Dentist posing with dental key and forceps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in a case. Half portrait of man wearing dark coat and striped vest, seated at a table, holding dental tools in both hands. Hand painted white highlights. Composite case with oval medallion, lined with black velvet, oval interior frame embossed with "Hale artist, no. 109 Wash'n St Boston," metal hinges, and push clasp.

did.unittitle: 17. Physician posing with his surgical kit and homeopathic medicines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in a case. Half portrait of man wearing dark jacket holding case of medicine on his lap and kit of surgical tools displayed on table. Brown case with gold embossed rose on cover, lined with red velvet with embossed floral motif, gilt trimed interior, quatrefoil interior frame, and two hook clasps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate tintype in a case. Half portrait of seated man wearing glasses, dark jacket, and vest holding an open case of medicines in glass vials in his right hand. Note inside front cover says "Homeopathic M.D." Dark brown case with embossed floral motif, front cover lined in red velvet with pressed plant motif, interior gilt trim, quatrefoil interior frame and one hook clasp.

did.unittitle: 19. Pharmacist with tools, chemical jars, and advertising placards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/2 plate ambrotype in a case. Image shows pharmacist seated at a table holding mortar and pestle; scale, funnel, and glass also displayed on table. Behind figure is painted backdrop and narrow shelf unit holding jars of chemicals with advertising placards and calendar attached. Brown case with raised garland and medallion motif and embossed gold trim and 2 hook clasps. Top interior of case lined with red velvet with embossed urn design; frame is copper with floral motif.

did.unittitle: 20. Frontier physician studying anatomy points to anatomical area on a human skull

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate ambrotype in a case. Half portrait of seated man with beard, wearing dark jacket and vest, holding a human skull, and with a large human femur bone on his lap. Brown case with mismatched covers, front lined in pink velvet with pressed design, quatrefoil interior frame, and one hook clasp. Small sticker on exterior bottom edge says "doctor and skull."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate tintype in a case. Two men with mustaches and beards wearing dark clothing seated at a table that holds a cadaver arm, skull, bone, and medical insturment kit. Man on the left is wearing sleeve protecters and dissecting cadaver arm with two tools. Man on right is holding large book on his lap. Brown case with embossed floral motif and gilt trim, interior of front case lined in dark blue velvet with pressed floral motif, interior edges trimmed in gilt, horizontal oval interior frame, and push button clasp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in a case. Half portrait of a man wearing dark jacket and lighter vest seated next to a table and holding a book in his left hand. The table holds books, a mortar and pastle, and glass jars of medicines. Hand tinted with white. Composite case with pastoral theme, front cover lined with burgundy velvet with pressed pattern, oval inner frame, metal hinges, and press clasp.

did.unittitle: 28. Medical student at a Philadelphia medical college

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate ambrotype in a case. Half portrait of seated man in dark jacket and vest with right hand resting on a human skull. Pink tinting on face. Backdrop has bookcase, mortar and pestle, and suspended human skeleton. Brown case with abstract motif, lined in embossed peach flocked paper, arched interior frame, and one hook clasp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/4 plate tintype in a case. Full portrait of two seated men; man on the left is wearing a light colored coat and holding a bone, the man on the right is wearing a dark colored coat and holding an open book. Pink hand tinting on faces. Dark brown case with floral motif, light pink velvet lining, decorated oval interior frame, and two hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 30. Dr. Beldon, New York City, posing with a skull with cigar in its' mouth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate ambrotype in a case. Half portrait of man wearing dark coat,vest, and hat next to a table holding a human skull and bones. The skull has a cigar in its' mouth. Dark brown case with grapes and bird motif, peach colored velvet lining with embossed pattern, gilt trim on interior edges, square interior frame, and 2 hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 31. Dr. Luther Abbot Hall, homeopathic physician, posing with medicine vials and his son, Luther, Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in a case. Half portrait of seated man with young boy standing beside him. Man is wearing dark jacket and vest, holding open case of medicine vials on his lap. Young boy is wearing dark buttoned up jacket. Hand tinted with white. Composite case with belt motif, lined with red velvet with embossed design, square interior frame, and push clasp.

did.unittitle: 32. Civil War contract surgeon in his tent

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 tintype plate in a case. Full portait of seated man inside a tent holding a book with a hat resting on his crossed leg. Interior of tent contains a table with books on top and on a lower shelf; chair with clothing and hat hanging on the back and bottles of medications on the seat; and spitoons, bottles, medical case, and tall boots on floor. Dark brown case with botanical motif, embossed peach velvet interior lining, rectangular interior frame, and 2 hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 33. Physician with his medical kit holding a prescription in a paper wrapper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate tintype plate in small frame. Half portrait of a seated man in a striped jacket holding an open case with vials of medicine on his lap. He is holding a small paper envelope in one hand and a small knife in the other hand. Dark wood frame with gold painted details, interior edge lined with red flocked paper, back of frame covered with marbled paper. Interior square frame. Small sticker on reverse says "MA22."

did.unittitle: 34. Foot amputation operation demonstration with saw and knives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate tintype in a case. Full portrait of three men demonstrating a foot amputation procedure. The "patient" sits on a table with a man standing on either side. All three men are wearing vests and white shirts, the standing men are wearing long aprons. The man on the right holds a large saw in front of the "patient" and the man's ankle in his other hand. The man on the left holds a large machete type blade in his right hand and grips a large bottle on the table in his left hand. The "patient" hold a large cleaver type knife upright with his left hand. There is a bucket in front of the table directly under the "patient's" foot. There is a painted backdrop behind the men. Brown case with horizontal gilt stripes, burgundy velvet interior with gilt trim, oval interior frame, and harp shaped clasp.

did.unittitle: 35. Comic operative scene with sign, surgeons hard at work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate tintype in a case. Full portrait of three men enacting a mock surgery. Patient is seated with leg stretched out in front of him, wearing hat with broken brim, holding an umbrella on his lap; surgeon is seated on left, wearing light colored hat over curly grey hair, holding patient's leg on the chair next to him, with knife in his mouth, and umbrella leaning against the back of his chair; surgeon's assistant is standing behind the patient, wearing a dark colored hat, holding a fan in one hand, other hand holds a bottle towards the patient's mouth. Sign on a stand behind the men reads "DI PLOMA, Ruch Med. College, Hon. Knowall MD, Purgandi & Physician, Paid 30.00." Dark brown case with star and bird motif, gilt trim on exterior and interior, embossed green velvet lining, oval interior frame, and two hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 36. Medical student posing with a skull and crossed bones

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/4 plate tintype in a case. 3 3/4 x 4 5/8 x 3/4 inches. Full portrait of a man holding a book and a rib bone seated next to table draped in dark fabric displaying skull and two crossed bones. Backdrop with trees and buildings behind. Brown case with quatrefoil design, interior gilt trim, red velvet lining, rectangular inner frame, and two hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 37. Pharmacist giving snuff to a patient

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/4 plate tintype in a case. 3 7/8 x 4 5/8 x 3/4 inches. Full portait of two seated men. Man on left is wearing a striped apron and loose coat and holding open container. Man on right is wearing dark jacket and top hat and taking a pinch of snuff from the container. Pink hand tinting on faces. Brown case with quatrefoil motif, interior gilt trim, red velvet lining, rectangular frame, and two hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 38. Homeopath with vials preparing a small dose

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate tintype in a case. Half portrait of seated man wearing dark jacket and vest, holding case with vials of medicine on his lap, and pouring contents from one vial into another. Brown composite case with oval medallion and floral motif, burgundy lining, oval interior frame, metal hinges, and push clasp.

did.unittitle: 39. Meade brothers studying medecine, Victor New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate tintype in a case. Full portrait of two men seated at a table with human skull on table and human bones on low stool. Man on left is wearing a light colored coat, resting his head on one hand. Man on right is holding an open book. Plain brown case lined with burgundy velvet, square interior frame, and push button clasp.

did.unittitle: 40. Frontier physician taking a patient's pulse

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/2 plate tintype in a case. Two seated men, one holds the other's wrist open pocket watch. Dark brown case with raised grape motif and gilt edges. Interior front cover lined in purple velvet with embossed decoration. Copper interior frame.

did.unittitle: 41. Pharmacist with the tools of his trade

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/4 plate tintype in a case. 3 3/4 x 4 5/8 x 3/4 inches. Three quarter portrait of man seated behind table holding mortar and pestle, scales, beaker, bottles with funnels, and spatula. Pink hand tinting on face. Brown case with gilt trim, light pink velvet lining, decorated oval interior frame, and two hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 42. Surgeon posing with a capital operating set

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate in a case with no front cover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate ambrotype in a case. Half portrait of seated man in darkjacket and vest holding a vial in one hand and resting other hand on a large upright book. Table next to the man holds the book, bottles of liquid, and a beaker. Brown case with center medallion and vine motif, lined in embossed purple velvet, interior gilt trim, quatrefoil interior frame, and two hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 45. Physician taking pulse of a woman on a fainting couch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate tintype in a case. Full portrait of kneeling physician taking the pulse of a woman on a fainting couch, surrounded by six other people. Man in boater hat standing by the woman's head is pouring something from a large bottle onto a cloth in his hand. Brown case with floral motif, gilt interior and exterior trim, lined in embossed purple velvet, with square interior frame, and two hook clasps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in a case. Half portrait of a man wearing dark jacket, vest, and plaid pants holding a scalpel in his left hand. Brown case with starburst motif, exterior and interior gilt trim, lined in embossed red velvet, with quatrefoil interior frame, and two hook clasps.

did.unittitle: 47. Painting of a surgeon holding a scalpel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1/6 plate daguerreotype in a case. Photograph of a painted portrait of a surgeon holding a scalpel. Embossed on interior velvet lining "Meade Brothers, 873(?) Broadway, New York." Dark brown case with birds and fountain motif, gilt interior and exterior trim, square interior frame, and two hook clasps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 16 slides, includes 4 colorized slides of skin conditions and 12 black and white slides of "cretinism", "mongolism", congenital deformities, an intestinal obstruction chart, and 2 images of the Pennsylvania Free Hospital at Philadelphia. Collection also includes a portion of a manuscript descrbing the Pennsylvania Free Hospital.

did.unittitle: Clara Barton, National First Aid Association

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 32 slides, includes images of the Clara Barton Ambulance Corps of the National First Aid Association of America training members, emergency responders, and the public, circa 1900-1910 and images of Clara Barton first aid initiatives in an unidentified war zone. 1 colored slide of American and Ambulance Corps flags, the remainder are black and white.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 slides, black and white diagrams and sections of eyeball affected by glaucoma.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 square slides, includes black and white illustrations and slide sections of eye and visual disruption from astigmatism. Labels indicate most slides were produced by Edward Ward of Manchester, who also produced scientific slides for microscopy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 slides, black and white, includes diagrams and text regarding structure of the eye, which appear to be copy images from a publication.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 3 1/4 inch square slides, black and white, includes images of multiple conditions affecting the eyes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 slides, black and white, includes 4 physician portraits and images of people with broken bones, orthopedic and neurological conditions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 slides, black and white, includes ulcers on legs and hands.

did.unittitle: Tuberculosis, Dr. Edmond F. Cody, New Bedford, Massachusetts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 slides, black and white, includes portraits of Robert Koch and Rene Theophile Laennec, statistics, instructions, images of a sanatorium, and patients.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 slides, includes 13 black and white images of children and adults infected with Tuberculosis and a parade float for dairy products and 1 colored illustration with instructions regarding fresh air and cleanliness. Slides were produced by multiple sources.

did.unittitle: Open reduction central fracture acetabulum plate fixation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 slides, black and white, includes images of X-rays and a patient.

scopecontent.p: 6 slides, black and white, includes images of metal splints, patients in traction, and a cast.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 slides, black and white, includes images and X-rays of a woman with an arm fracture, before and after cast is applied to arm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Slides were received grouped by topic, most are labeled, images include patients and organs or tumors surgically removed, surgical diagrams, and illustrations. Earlier slides are black and white, later include color images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 slides, labeled "resection with aseptic end-to-end anastomosis for carcinoma of colon. Slides of paper." includes diagrams and illustrations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 slides, black and white diagrams with 1 slide showing photos of procedure.

scopecontent.p: 6 slides, black and white photographs of multiple patients.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 slides, black and white, includes X-rays, tissue, diagram, table.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 slides, black and white images from 1 patient (named) include tissue sample, 2 x-rays, removed tumor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 slides, includes 3 color images of patients dated 1952, black and white illustrations, tissue samples, 2 black and white images of patients dated 1928 and 1932.

scopecontent.p: 3 slides, black and white images of 1 patient, 1 thorax X-ray, X-ray and photo of hand.

odd.head: General
odd.p: This series is unprocessed. Contact the Medical Historical Library at historical.library@yale.edu to request materials that can not be requested using Archives at Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 179 cabinet photographs (cabinet cards) organized into categories: rare (0001-0034), assorted medical (0035-0063), physicians (0064-0079), patients/medical conditions (0080-0106), conjoined twins (01017-0117), Black patients/medical conditions (0118-0121), opthalmology patients (0122-0138), nurses (0139-0151), circus sideshow performers (0152-0170), physicians, larger format (0171-0174).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 84 photographic prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 148 items including 120 postcards and 28 photographic prints in multiple formats and sizes. Postcards include sanatoriums (0001-0009), asylums (0010-0019), military hospitals (0020-0023), medical schools (0024-0033), specialty hospitals (0034-0043), general/state hospitals (00044-01030, and hospitals outside the United States (0104-0120).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 204 cartes-de-visite. Items are organized by the following topics: important assorted medical (#1-20), great medical scenes (#21-28), posing with skulls (#29-35), Psychiatric Association Images (#36-42), tumors (#43-49), early hearing aids (#50-53), hydrocephalus (#54-57), Silvester Roe, photographer and magnetic healer (#58-62), patients/medical problems (#63-71), disabilities: hand and leg abnormalities (#72-79), disability (#73-103), patients with doctor photographer (#104-114), major European physicians (#115-127), European doctors 2 (#128-131), minor physicians and doctor photographers (#132-137), important American doctors (#138-148), British and European scientists (#149-152), American physicians (#153-179), nurses (#180-191), and pharmacy/ miscellaneous medical (#192-200).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 401 photographic postcards and picture postcards. Cards are organized by the following topics: war (items #1-10), diseased states (#11-39), congenital deformities (#40-46), advertisements (#47-52), doctors (#53-91), nurses (#92-114), chiropractic schools/treatments (#115-121), great images (#122-217), humor (#218-226), medical treatments (#227-258), veterinary (#259-264), operating room (#265-289), and hospitals/asylums (#290-401).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 23 gelatin silver prints, 7 x 5 inches. Photographer: unknown. Location: unknown. Description: doctors and nurses conducting an abdominal surgery at a hospital. The images document the patient receiving anesthesia, nurses prepping tools, and doctors using various instruments over the patient's body. Subject: operative scenes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 16 gelatin silver prints.

did.unittitle: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists cervical cone display, Atlantic City, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 gelatin silver prints.

did.unittitle: American Women's Hospital No. 1, France, World War I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 gelatin silver prints, 6.6 x 4.75 inches. Photographer: E. Belval. Location: France, either Neufmotiers or Luzancy. Description: photographs of scenes at the American Women's Hospital in France circa 1915. Includes exterior shots of the hospital, ambulance, surgical scenes, nurses and doctors with patients in wards, and other scenes around the hospital. All of the medical professionals pictured are women. Subject: medicine and war; hospitals and wards; nursing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 cartes-de-visite, 2.5 x 4.25 inches. Photographer: unknown. Location: New York City. Description: portraits of Bellevue physicians in the 1880s. Some items have names noted on the reverse including A. Doremut, J. Bryant, Herman Biggs, Alexander Mutt, and Austin Flint. Subject: pioneers and innovators; hospitals and wards.

did.unittitle: Bellevue Training School for Male Nurses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 18 gelatin silver prints, 5.25 x 4.25 inches, mounted. Photographer: unknown. Location: New York City. Description: scenes includes exterior shots of the hospital, posed group portraits of nurses, and training scenes around the hospital. Female nurses are present in addition to the male nurses. Subjects: nursing; hospitals and wards.

did.unittitle: Reed Bontecou Civil War injuries teaching collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographe: Reed B. Bontecou (Reed Brockway), MD, 1824-1907. Includes 3 cartes-de-visite and large format prints documenting civil war soldier's injuries and recovery. Large prints are annotated with red ink to highlight wounds and trajectory of bullets. Cartes-de-visite were originally gifted to the United States Army Museum by Bontecou and have registration numbers, Bontecou's stamp, and other information on reverse sides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 albumen prints, multiple sizes, mounted, 6.5 x 8.5 inches. Photographer: unknown. Location: Boston, Massachusetts. Description: various scenes around the Boston City Hospital, including many exterior views, wards and operating rooms. Doctors, nurses, and patients are visible in several images. Subject: hospitals and wards; nursing.

did.unittitle: Camp Dix Base Hospital, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 gelatin silver prints. Includes nurses in wards, studying, preparing medication, in laboratory, exercising, in the eye clinic, and one surgical scene.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 30 items; 29 gelatin silver prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 gelatin silver prints, 4.2 x 3.25 inches. Photographer: unknown. Location: unknown. Description: collection of ten differentiate circumcision surgery scenes. Subject: operative scenes.

did.unittitle: Pierre and Marie Curie postcards, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 photographic postcards, 5.5 x 2.5 inches. Photographer: unknown. Location: France. Description: includes a portrait of Marie Curie, a portrait of Pierre Curie, a portrait of both at a lab bench, and a photograph of Pierre Curie at La Sorbonne surrounded by his students. Specialty: radiology, oncology, radium therapy. Subject: pioneers and innovators; laboratories and doctors' offices.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 photographic postcards, 3.5 x 5.5 inches. Photographer: unknown. Location: Spain. Description: series chronicling a doctor's visit to a family. The postcards are numbered 1-10 and tell a chronological story. Subject: therapy and treatment.

did.unittitle: Female chiropractor (Palmer Chiropractic School) personal photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 87 items; 86 gelatin silver prints in several sizes and a signed letter. Most images are of people engaged in leisure activities including hiking, near or in bodies of water, in gardens, and on a boat. A few items are labeled "Devil's Lake State Park, Wisconsin."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 gelatin silver prints.

did.unittitle: Carlos Finlay, M.D., yellow fever research, Havana, Cuba

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 gelatin silver prints, 4.25 x 6.5inches, mounted. Photographer: Carlos Finlay. Location: Havana, Cuba. Description: photographs of microscope slides from Dr. Carlos Finlay's study of yellow fever in Cuba. Each image of blood infected with yellow fever is annotated to indicate day of the patient's infection the blood was taken (i.e. 5th day), and whether or not the case was fatal. Also includes photographs from other glass slides. Specialty: infectious diseases. Subject: medical specialties; disease and pathology; pioneers and innovators.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 prints removed from unidentified volumes of the journal Archives of Pediatrics , multiple sizes on 5.5 x 9 inch pages. Photographer: unknown. Location: various. Description: portraits of "Eminent Pediatrists of the World." Each page contains a portrait, the doctor's signature, and a month and year. Specialty: pediatrics. Subject: medical specialties; pioneers and innovators.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 gelatin silver prints, 8 x 5 inches. Photographer: unknown. Location: unknown. Description: posed group portraits of doctors and nurses in various parts of a hospital, including an operating room, lounge, and front steps. Also includes an exterior shot. Subject: hospitals and wards; nursing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 gelatin silver prints, 8.5 x 6.5 inches. Includes group portraits of nurses and doctors, hospital wards, staff dining room, and 1 building exterior. Photographer: unknown. Location: unknown. Description: recovering in wards. Subject: hospitals and wards; nursing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 gelatin silver prints, including annotated proof sheets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 gelatin silver prints, one mounted. Includes building exterior, wards, and group portrait of doctors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 123 gelatin silver prints, multiple sizes. Photographer: unknown. Location: Baltimore, Maryland. Description: surgical photographs from Johns Hopkins, pre and post operative images of multiple conditions including lip reconstruction, head wounds, and endocrine and neurological cases; x-rays; surgical instruments; and dissected organs. Subject: disease and pathology; therapy and treatments; hospitals and wards.

did.unittitle: Lernt helfen : ein Raumbildwerk der ersten Hilfe für Laienhelfer : mit 60 Raumbildern / Helmut Zapp. (Learn to Help: a three-dimensional first aid guide for lay helpers), Germany

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 60 stereoscopic photographs, 5.1 x 2.3 inches. Photographer: Raumbild-Verlag Otto Schonstein. Location: Germany. Description: series of 60 stereoviews (originally packaged with a viewer in a hard cover). Most scenes show people aiding injured people, a few are x-rays or close up shots of injuries. The back of each stereoview has a description of the scene. Subject: anatomy and education.

did.unittitle: Lincoln School for Nurses, Bronx, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 gelatin silver prints, multiple sizes. Photographer: unknown. Location: New York City. Description: photographs of Black nurses in uniform and in regular clothing, some posed on steps of the school. Subject: nursing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 gelatin silver prints, mounted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 cartes-de-visites, 2.5 x 4.25 inches. Photographer: Alex Gardner (5 items), Phillip and Solomon (1 item), Maurice Farrington (1 item), and unknown. Location: New York City. Description: images of Dr. Edward Maynard, dentist and firearms inventor, as well as his family. Majority of portraits were made by photographers in Washington D.C. Specialty: dentistry. Subject: medical specialties; pioneers and innovators.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 cabinet photographs (cabinet cards), 4.2 x 6.5 inches. Photographer: unknown. Location: Burlington, Vermont. Description: portraits of medical school doctors with names noted on the backs of cards. Subject: anatomy and education.

did.unittitle: J. Meeker M.D., Morristown, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 35 cartes-de-visites, 2.5 x 4.25 inches, multiple sizes. Photographer: Gurney (11 items), Brady (2 items), D. Clark, Lacey, and others. Location: Morristown, New Jersey. Description: portraits from the family album of Dr. J. W. Meeker. Subject: pioneers and innovators.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 18 photographic negatives, multiple sizes. Photographer: unknown. Location: unknown. Description: negatives of unidentified patients, mainly portraits, also includes a photograph of skin lesions, some diseased organs, and medical instruments. Subject: disease and pathology.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 cabinet photographs (cabinet cards), 4.2 x 6.4 inches. Photographer: Charles Lainer. Location: 715 Market Street, San Francisco, California. Description: images of a young male patient during a neurological exam. Includes reflex testing, etc. Specialty: neurology. Subject: medical specialties; therapy and treatments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 37 gelatin silver prints, multiple dimensions. Photographer: unknown. Location: New Jersey. Description: includes images of patients recovering in wards, adults and children with physical abnormalities, and "diagnosis photographs" of patients. Photographs of leg and foot problems, such as turned feet, knock knees, and hip problems are featured. Includes images of white and Black patients. Specialty: orthopedics. Subject: hospitals and wards; disease and pathology; therapy and treatments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 33 cabinet photographs (cabinet cards), 4.25 x 6.5 inches. Photographer: Dana of New York. Location: New York City. Description: portraits, some have doctor's names and hospital appointments on the back. Subject: anatomy and education.

did.unittitle: New York State Mental Health Exhibition collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 gelatin silver prints, 10 x 8 inches, mounted, 14 x 12 inches. Photographer: Cunningham Studios, Utica, New York. Location: New York State. Description: patients at the Utica State Hospital participating in different exercise and crafting classes. Men and women are treated separately. Men's classes include exercising, sewing, weaving, and woodworking; women's classes include knitting, needlepoint, and doll and rug making. Some captions denote a "grade" indicating the severity of mental illness of the patients. One photograph is of patients performing The Merchant of Venice at the Central Islip State Hospital. Specialty: mental health. Subject: medical specialties; therapy and treatments; hospitals and wards. Prints are numbered 2-12, there is no print 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 63 items: 53 gelatin silver prints, 10 glass negatives.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Collection is restricted until conservation treatment is completed.

did.unittitle: New York University physicians, Gurney collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 cartes-de-visites, 2.5 x 4.25 inches. Photographer: Gurney. Location: New York City. Description: Portraits, some with name of the doctor noted on the back. Subject: hospitals and wards; pioneers and innovators.

did.unittitle: Nurses personal collection, Cook County Hospital, Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 65 gelatin silver prints of various sizes. Includes images of nurses and patients in a variety of settings, some printed in Texas and Indiana.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 19 items; 15 stereoscopic photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 photographic prints, multiple formats, multiple dimensions. Photographer: unknown. Location: France. Description: personal photographs of Dr. Victor Morax. Includes postcards and photographs, mostly group shots of family and friends. Dr. Morax was one of the world's leading ophthalmologists, famous for his work in bacteriology, notably the discovery of Morax-Axenfeld diplobacilli. Specialty: opthalmology. Subject: medical specialties.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 photographic prints, multiple formats, multiple dimensions. Photographer: unknown. Location: Haifa, Palestine (now Israel). Description: includes portraits of doctors, and doctors examining patients. Specialty: ophthalmology. Subject: laboratories and doctors' offices; therapy and treatments; operative scenes.

did.unittitle: Ophthalmology exhibition, Manhattan Eye Ear and Throat Hospital

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 items: 10 photographic prints, approximately 13 x 17 inches, and 1 copy of The continuing story of Manhattan Eye, Ear and Throat Hospital: 77th annual report . Photographer: Tad Horton. Location: New York City. Description: prints from exhibit at the hospital, including surgical scenes and patient exams, in large format, mainly focusing on ophthalmology. Specialty: ophthalmology. Subject: medical specialties; therapy and treatments; operative scenes.

did.unittitle: Palmer Chiropractic School women's collection, Davenport, Iowa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 19 gelatin silver prints. Photographs document Palmer Chiropractic School Lyceum parade, unveiling of new bust of Dr. Palmer, faculty, nurses, and Palmer family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 glass plate negatives.

did.unittitle: Pre and post-operative cancer patients, Allentown, Pennsylvania

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 108 gelatin silver prints, multiple sizes. Photographer: unknown. Location: Allentown, Pennsylvania. Description: includes treatment of oral wounds, mastectomies, and other conditions. Collection includes male (45 photos) and female (63 photos) cancer patients. Subject: disease and pathology; therapy and treatments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 gelatin silver prints. Images include Red Cross clinic boat, medical staff treating patients, education sessions, laboratory, and midwife training.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 gelatin silver prints, 10 x 8 inches. Photographer: Syndication International. Location: Russian Republic of Georgia (now Georgia). Description: rabbits and monkeys being used for a lung cancer study. Pictures include animals with "duck bill" masks strapped to their faces, which each have an opening with a large cigarette sticking out. Scientists are seen lighting cigarettes and checking on the animals. Also includes photographs of monkeys in a zoo-like habitat, which may be where they live when not part of the experiment. Subject: disease and pathology; therapy and treatments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 mounted albumen prints, varying sizes. Photographer William Schroeder, M.D., of Brooklyn, New York. Includes images of monuments to Benjamin Silliman and Ephraim McDowell and composite portraits of Brooklyn Pathological Society, Anatomical and Surgical Society, Medica-chirugical Society, and Long Island College Hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 platinum prints, 7 x 9 inches, mounted. Photographer: unknown. Location: New York City. Description: person showing just the face, or face and partial torso. A few images show two people, siblings or mother and child. Some portraits note when during the patient's illness the photograph was taken (i.e. 6 hours before death). Specialty: infectious diseases. Subject: disease and pathology; therapy and treatments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 16 gelatin silver prints, 10 x 8 inches. Photographer: United States Army. Location: unknown. Description: scientists preparing serum, pictured during different parts of the creation process in laboratory with various instruments and measures, pressurized containers, and refrigeration unit. Some of the photographs list the step they are on, or the equipment being used. All scientists are male. Subject: therapy and treatments; laboratories and doctors' offices; medicine and war.

did.unittitle: United States General Hospital Number 30, Plattsburgh, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 photographic postcards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 gelatin silver prints.

did.unittitle: World War II Australian military medical series, New Guinea

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7 gelatin silver prints, multiple sizes. Photographer: (Australian) Department of Information. Location: New Guinea. Description: Australian military medical doctors, includes operative scenes, makeshift triage stations and wards, soldiers burying the dead, and dead soldiers in rubble. Subject: medicine and war; operative scenes.

did.unittitle: World War II blood transfusion collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 gelatin silver prints.

did.unittitle: Hugh H. Young, M.D., copy print collection

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

odd.head: General
odd.p: This series is unprocessed. Contact the Medical Historical Library at historical.library@yale.edu to request materials that can not be requested using Archives at Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11.75 x 14.5 inches; 306 photographic prints, 3 x 4 inches, 2 x 3 inches. Japanese lacquered cover with image of Mt. Fuji and abalone inlay, heavy black paper leaves, multiple images per page. Album created by noncommissioned officer in medical corps, includes photographs of the clinic, medical dressing station, and soldier's life in Japan, including trips to Hiroshima and 8 colorized tourist photographs of Kobe. Also includes one photograph of dentists at work. Some photographs are captioned in ink on surface.

did.unittitle: Berkley Gymnasium spinal curvature and posture logbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 10.5 x 8.25 inches; 10 photographic prints, 5 x 7 inches; patient intake cards, posture change plots, and labratory test result reports. Leather covered notebook, 252 lined pages, contains hand written notes and documention of scoliosis patients recorded by M. K. Wallin, M.D. Examination cards, lab result reports, drawings recording changes in spinal curvature, and photographs are interleaved with notes. Some photographs include captions on reverse.

did.unittitle: Boxer Rebellion military hospital album, China

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7 x 10 inches; 53 items: photographic prints, cyanotypes, photographic postcards. Gray paper cover with partial border and "Photographs" in raised green; 16 grey paper leaves; bound with string. Sticker inside back cover: "Mfd. By the Heinn Specialty Co., Milwaukee, Wis., Badger Albums 03." Photographs include medical tents, doctors, patient care, priests, military scenes, and locations including Peking (now Beijing), Mt. Wang-Sho, and Tientsin (also known as Tianjin) during the Boxer Rebellion in China.

did.unittitle: Cambridge Instrument Company, electro cardiology equipment album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11.25 x 9.5 inches; 32 photographic prints. Brown faux leather binder with 2 prong metal attachment bar. Half of album contents in plastic sleeves, remainder is tipped in or hole punched. Materials in album include photographic prints of people, electrocardiology equipment, and written material; glossy and matte paper with images and text; cardboard leaf with electrocardiograph printouts attached; and reproductions of typed documents on Cambridge Instrument Company letterhead stationery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page dimensions: 12 x 9 inches. 5 photographic prints, 4.5 x 3 inches. 1 disbound black paper album page. Photographs of Cardinal Spellman with a wounded soldier in a ward and with doctors and military staff in a ward and exiting a building.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 14.5 x 11.5 inches; 95 photographic prints, 5 x 7 inches. Maroon leather album with art nouveau style metal decorations on front cover, leaves are pale green board with frame openings. Album created by Alexander Lacassagne, Leon, France. Includes portraits of various dermatological diseases with handwritten notes.

did.unittitle: Chadwick-Herns, Ava V., Battle Creek Sanitarium album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 6.75 x 9.75; multiple trimmed photographic prints. Printed publication with brown paper cover, enclosed in green suede outer cover, secured with suede string. Title on brochure cover is "Battle Creek Sanitarium" embossed in silver gilt with oval print illustration of hospital exterior adhered to cover. Brochure published by the sanitarium used by sanitarium employee Ava V. Chadwick as a personal photograph album. Contains handwritten notes and inscriptions to Chadwick from sanitarium patients and staff, many accompanied by small portrait photographs, also 1 tipped in note and photograph of exterior of institution adhered to title page.

did.unittitle: Colonie Marine 1925: Fondazione Citta Fiume per la Colonie Infantili Marine et Montane . Pediatric tuberculosis sanitarium, Fiume, Italy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 8.75 x 6.75 inches; 17 photographic prints, 4.5 x 6.25 inches and 3.25 x 5.25 inches. Gray cover with "Colonie Marine, 1925" and "Fondazione Citta Fiume per la Colonie Infantili Marine et Montane" printed in embossed gold; leaves are gray paper. Inscription on first page: "A John J. Freschi Presidente del Comitante, Corciata per i bambini tubereolotici di Fiume in segno di viva gratitudine, la Fondazione "Citta di Fiume" per colonie Marine e Montane offre. Fiume Ottobre 1925." Album depicts treatments for children with tuberculosis, including swimming in the ocean, heliotherapy, dining outside, napping under trees, being weighed and measured, taking medication, and attending class in open sided tents. Inscriptions written in ink below photographs are in Italian.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11.25 x 15.5 iches; 89 photographic prints in bound album, 31 prints on unbound album pages. Album covers are missing, black paper leaves. Photographer: Trafton and unknown. Detailed documentation of the conquest of yellow fever in Havana including sanitary efforts, medical procedures, laboratory studies, administrative efforts, and vaccine preparations. Album includes list of contents.

did.unittitle: Considérations sur la Rééducation Professionelle Dans les Industries du Bâtiment . Post World War I French disability rehabilitation report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 4.25 x 6.5 inches; 13 photographic prints and typed report. Library binding, disbound pages of manilla paper, and tipped in report on translucent paper. Photographs are glued to pages with typed text. Official research presentation to the French Government showing how to rehabilitate soldiers with leg amputations into various occupations. Photographs show healed amputations, prosthetics, and soldiers wearing prosthetics at work. Report is signed by C. Vallée. Album is in French.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11.25 x 9 inches; 21 photographic prints. Yellow paper cover album laced with blue string, 21 medium weight off-white paper leaves. Images and captions are printed on pages. Includes prints of photographs of autopsy, surgical scenes, and emergency room treatment.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This item is restricted pending preservation treatment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 10 x 12.75. 14 photographic prints, multiple sizes. 3 disbound black paper leaves of a photograph album. Album title and captions in white ink. Photographs include bacteriology lab, students, doctors, and building exteriors. Also includes 2 photographs of a visit to Yale.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Page dimensions: 9.5 x 12.75; 40 photographic prints, multiple sizes. 4 disbound off-white paper leaves from one or more photograph albums. Images include nurses and doctors attending patients and at rest, surgical scenes, traction apparatus, wards, and patients.

did.unittitle: Héon, Dr. Bernard, X-ray album, Avranches, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 10 x 13 inches; 46 photographic prints, multiple sizes, including 1 cyanotype. Red cloth over board album covers, hard board leaves. Album contains X-ray photographs of human body parts and some animals. Photographs are glued to album pages, includes some handwritten descriptions in French. Several photographs stamped "Doctor Bernard Heon Avranches" on front or reverse.

did.unittitle: LaFerte, Dr. Daniel Antoin, pediatric orthopedic album, Volume 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 10.5 x 8.5 inches; 21 cabinet photographs (cabinet cards), cyanotypes (photographs). Brown covers, leaves are off white board with frame openings. Photographer: C. Eisenheart. Individual portraits of patients with names and dates written in ink below. Includes letter from Wes Cowam with doctor's information: Dr. Daniel Antoin La Ferte began practice in 1871 at Harper General and Children's Free Hospital, he died in 1924. He was a pioneer in hip surgery.

did.unittitle: LaFerte, Dr. Daniel Antoin, pediatric orthopedic album, Volume 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11.25 x 9.25 inches; 25 cabinet photographs (cabinet cards). Brown covers, leaves are off white board with frame openings. Photographer: Watson. Individual portraits of patients with names and dates written in ink below.

did.unittitle: Faulkner, James, M.D., Harvard Medical Unit, World War I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11 x 14 inches; 469 photographic prints, typed documents, ephemera, newspaper clippings. Album created by Dr. James Faulkner, documents activities of medical unit in France, training in the United States, and general war scenes in France and Germany. Photographs are captioned with handwritten descriptions or captions printed in the image. Documents include discharge papers. Also includes program from Harvard University Unit, 2nd Contingent, visit to Oxford signed by William Osler.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This item is restricted pending preservation treatment.

did.unittitle: Fulton, Mrs. Dr. A. M., Women's Medical College, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 9 x 12 inches; 65 photographic prints; cartes-de-visite (card photographs), cabinet photographs (cabinet cards), and 13.4 x 6.5 inch photographic prints. Dark brown leather cover with metal clasp, leaves are off-white board with frame openings. Gold embossed inscription reads: "Presented to Mrs. Dr. A. M. Fulton by friends in London. February 1874." Album of first woman graduate of Women's Medical College. Includes 52 cartes-de-visite of physicians and nurses, cartes-de-visite and cabinet photographs (cabinet cards) of English royalty, and large format photographic prints of British landscape. Some photographs are inscribed on reverse, some are captioned on album pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11 x 15 inches. 27 photographic prints, multiple sizes. Disbound black paper leaves, 2 full and 2 partial from a photograph album. Includes photographs of Pirquet Klinik, Kaiser and Kaiserin Friedrich Kinderkranken Haus, and tourist photographs of Berlin with captions written in white ink. Images include wards, preparing food, patients, children napping outside, doctors, and nurses.

did.unittitle: Havlena, Captain William, M.D., Medical Corp, 343 Fighter Group, Alaska
Homeopathic medical school carte de visite album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 5.5 x 8 inches; 79 cartes-de-visite (card photographs). Leather covered 3 dimensional covers with gold embossed patterning, 4 ceramic buttons on top and bottom covers, 2 metal and ceramic hinged hasp closures. Leaves of paper covered board have frame openings and slots for inserting carte de visite photographs, gold borders around frame and sides of page, edges of leaves are gilded. Each page holds the portraits of two doctors, many with names inscribed on front or reverse. Includes two photographs of portraits of C. F. Hahnemann, founder of homeopathic medicine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 5.75 x 8.5 inches; 50 items: 46 photographic prints, 2 drawings, 1 printed photograph (paper copy), 1 certificate. Black cloth over board covers, leaves were secured with black string (album was unbound for preservation). Album includes text and photographs documenting hookworm disease parasite, symptoms, causes, efforts to eradicate disease in Jamaica, and treatment of patients. Includes photographs of microscopic views of the parasite, people infected with Hookworm, public education events, patients before and after treatment, Jamaica Hookworm Commission staff, documents and signs, and the transcript of a patient testimonial letter.

did.unittitle: Jewish doctor studying medicine in Vienna

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 8.25 x 12 inches; 197 photographic prints. 42 leaves of dark brown paper alternate with patterned translucent paper. Album created by a Jewish doctor from New York, documents several trips to Europe, including medical studies in Vienna 1932-1933, and vacations in the United States. Contains photographs from medical school, autopsies, anatomy institute, travels, and family members. Most photographs are captioned with names, dates, and locations.

did.unittitle: Kriegszahnklinik der IV. Armee in Lublin . German army maxillofacial surgery album, World War I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 10 x 13 inches; 43 photographic prints, 4.5 x 6.5 inches, 4 x 5.75 inches. Green cloth covered album, leaves of dark green board, and brown patterned end papers. Title "Kriegszahnklinik der IV Armee in Lublin" (War Dental Clinic of the Fourth Army in Lublin). Photographs mounted one or two per page. Images of medical staff, patients being treated, medical equipment and facilities. Images include soldiers who have had reconstructive surgery on their face and jaws sitting next to a cast of their injures. Also includes images of the city of Lublin, including Jewish areas.

did.unittitle: London Military Hospitals of the Grand Duchess George of Russia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 9.25 x 12.25 inches; 193 photographic prints, multiple sizes including 3 x 5 inches, 3 x 4 inches, and 8 x 10 inches; 1 Christmas card and 1 newspaper clipping. Purple leather cover with gold embosseded interior, hard board leaves. Portrait glued to inside of front cover. Photographs of wounded British soldiers from various military hospitals including Heather Hill hospital, Tewit Hall Hospital, Hospital H.I.H. Grand Duchess George of Russia, and St. Nicholas Hospital. Includes hand written captions, some on reverse of prints. Inscription on first page reads "From the staff and wounded men at St. Nicholas Hospital, [?] 1915, Harragate."

did.unittitle: Mackinnon, Ethel F., nurse's album, World War I, England

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7.5 x 10.25 inches; 60 items: 51 photographic prints, 9 photographic postcards. Brown cloth covered board cover, dark brown board leaves. Inscription on the front endpaper reads "Ethel F. Mackinnon, Greenback House, Greenick(?), 11th Novr., 1919." Includes photographs of nurses, soldiers, wards, hospital lounges, and recreation rooms at Southwood and Springburn hospitals, some captioned with names and positions below. Also incudes postcard of rail car fitted out for tranport of wounded British soldiers.

did.unittitle: MacKinnon, Lillian, Anglo-American Hospital superintendent, Havana, Cuba

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11 x 8.75 inches; 10 photographic prints, multiple sizes; clippings; and ephemera. Unbound black paper leaves, no cover. Album created by Lillian MacKinnon, superintendent of the Anglo-American Hospital in Havana, Cuba. Clippings discuss MacKinnon's career in South American and the Carribean, hospital organization and care, and Anglo-American society in Havana. Ephemera includes thank you notes from former patients and colleagues, event invitations from ambassadors and political figures, and material from her work with the Red Cross.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 6.25 x 7.5 inches; 26 prints, adhered to album pages. Maroon fabric cover with black leather corners and spine, leaves are off-white board. Includes microscopic views of human tissue.

title.part: Missouri State Sanatorium at Mount Vernon Mo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 5.75 x 8.25 inches; 85 photographic prints, multiple dimensions. Black cloth cover with title written in gold ink and double barred cross in red ink; black paper leaves. Sticker inside back cover reads "The IDEAL, to duplicate order No. 2305B, Made by the J.L. Hanson Co., Chicago." Photographs of Missouri State Sanatorium at Mount Vernon, Missouri. Includes grounds, building exteriors, dining hall, wards, patients, nurses, and doctors.

title.part: National Drug Company Biologicals and Pharmaceuticals, Philadelphia, U.S.A.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 10.25 x 13.75 inches; 21 large photographic prints. Leather cover. Each photograph preceded by description printed on a translucent page. Album documents production of tetanus and smallpox vaccines at Swiftwater Antitoxin and Vaccine Laboratories of the National Drug Company in the Pocono Mountains, Pennsylvania. Includes photographs of facility exteriors, stables and animal pens, sterilizing room, laboratories, storage of "biologicals", collection of sera by vacuum, and purification process.

scopecontent.p: Album dimension: 6.5 x 9 inches; 17 photographic prints, multiple sizes. 4 black paper leaves from a photograph album. Includes photographs of nurses in uniform performing work, interior and exterior shots of nurses posing in groups, and 4 photographs of women not in uniform.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7.25 x 10.25 inches; 161 photographic prints, photographs are glued to album pages. Brown cloth cover, black paper leaves. Album created by a nurse who worked at the Jewish Hospital in Philadelphia and other institutions. Photographs include nurses, patients, doctors, hospital building exteriors and grounds, World War I soldiers, vacations and family.

did.unittitle: Nurse's album, Memorial Hospital, New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7.75 x 5.5 inches; 102 photographic prints, multiple format, including cyanotype (photographs). Red leather cover with black paper leaves, front cover missing. Contains photographs from multiple states in the U.S.A. and Japan. Includes all female surgical scene and nurses attending patients at Memorial Hospital [for Women and Children], Brooklyn New York; vacations; family scenes; and homes. Photographs cut into various shapes, arranged, some are individually labeled.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7.75 x 12.5 inches; 49 photographic prints. Grey paper cover; 20 off-white paper leaves with irregular faux parchment edges, printed gray border, inscribed "Marilu" in blue pencil at bottom of page. 2 or 3 photographs are adhered to each page. Back of photos stamped "Photo Marilu. Cabildo 1708 - U. T. 73-8291" and have control numbers written in pencil. Album contains images of patient examinations with pediatricians and nurse.

did.unittitle: Pfahler, George E., M.D., Old Blockley Hospital album, Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 5.75 x 7.5 inches; 26 photographic prints, 4.75 x 3.75 inches. Gray fabric covered paper covers with "photographs" printed in gilt on front; leaves are off white paper. Album created by George E. Pfahler M.D., a physician from Philadelphia. Each page contains 1 photograph with caption. Photographs include hospital staff, wards, events, and doctors and nurses socializing. Includes image of operating room and new ambulance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7.5 x 10.75 inches; 59 photographic prints, multiple sizes, bulk 4.25 x 2.5 inches and part of student identity card. Black patterned paper cover with 2 raised geometric decorations on front cover; heavy dark gray paper leaves. Album created by female student of Swedish Farmaceutska Institute. Images depict students and instructors with microscopes, in laboratories and classrooms, posed in groups, and photographs of individual female students in laboratories. Also includes personal images of home and family.

did.unittitle: Plastic surgery case studies, New Jersey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 14.5 x 17 inches; 541 photographic prints, multiple sizes, bulk 3.25 x 4.25 inches. Photographs are adhered to black paper leaves. Plastic surgeon case study records for male and female patients with facial and skull injuries. Includes a list of patients taped to the front page of the book and photographs documenting patients pre, during, and post-treatment.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11.75 x 10.25 inches; 134 photographic prints, 4 x 6 and 3.5 x 5.5 inches. Photographs are in vinyl 3 ring binder with photograph sleeves. Some photographs include handwritten descriptive notes on reverse. Album documents pneumonic plague in Fu Chia Dieu, China. Photographs include doctors, hospital buildings, mass graves, and buildings burning. Also includes photograph of Dr. Paul Hoffkine at the Plague Hospital.

did.unittitle: Post World War I German nurse's album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 4.25 x 6 inches; 46 photographic prints. Black leather album cover with a logo that reads "Precision" on the front; leaves are photographs adhered to canvas, album secured by 2 brass screws. Contains 13 images of machines and 33 images of hospitals, including photographs of several x-ray machines in use by physicians, and list of hospitals using the machines.

did.unittitle: Red Cross Blood Drive album, World War II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 14.5 x 12.25 inches; 19 gelatin silver prints, newspaper clippings, Red Cross publications, reports, and letter. Red cover, 26 off white paper leaves. Scrapbook from World War II Red Cross nurses collecting blood in the United States for the war effort abroad. Scrapbook created by Red Cross nurse Betty Weatherington. Inscription on first page reads "Red Cross Blood Drive Center, 925 S. Western Ave, Los Angeles, Calif." Photographs include nurse portraits and nurses collecting blood. Red Cross material includes "What Can I Do - The Citizen's Handbook for War." Photographs are captioned in red ink.

did.unittitle: Remembrance of the 14th International Medicine Congress
Royhan, W. H., Ambulance Company 331, World War I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 10.1 x 6.9 inches; 92 gelatin silver prints, multiple sizes, one 7 x 9 inch print. Also includes a poem, 1 mark bill, diagram of an ambulance, and amusement company program. Embossed leather-like cover, 29 black paper leaves. Personal photograph album of Mr. W.H. Royhan from Akron, Ohio, who served as a soldier in World War I. Photographs include formal portraits, images from the field, groups of soldiers, ambulances, parades, boat trips, funerals, and war hospitals. Back of the album has a large format formal portrait of a soldier, presumably W. H. Royan, taken in 1919 in Ashtabula, Ohio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 14 x 17.25 inches; 16 photographic prints. Fabric covered board cover, 16 unbound leaves of heavy board secured in 4 flap enclosure. Each leaf has one large photographic print adhered, enclosed by printed border. Prints depict assembled photocollages (photographic compositions) of 20-24 photographs, including portraits of medical specialists, images of wards, operations, and classrooms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7 x 9.25 inches; 300 photographic prints, various sizes. 22 unbound pages. Each page contains a printed collage of portraits and images, including hospital exteriors, surgical scenes, dissection hall, laboratories, and patient treatment.

did.unittitle: Schönborn, Siegfried and Krieger, Hans, Klinischer Atlas der Nervenkrankheiten , 71 plates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Disbound pages from an incomplete volume of Klinischer Atlas der Nervenkrankheiten (Clinical Atlas of Nerve Diseases) by Siegfried Schönborn and Hans Krieger. Publication is in German. Plates include muscular dystrophy, neurogenic muscular atrophy, myotonia, paralysis, palsy, neuritis, polio, syringomyelia, post-traumatic stress (post-traumatic hysteria), muscle spasm, nervous tics, chorea, tetany, and athetosis.

did.unittitle: Stickney, Dr. E. P., Arlington Hospital album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7.25 x 10.25 inches; 17 photographic prints. Black cloth covered album, 25 black paper leaves. "Dr. E. P. Stickney" embossed in gold on front cover, sticker inside back cover: "Devine & Parsons, Commercial Photographers, P. O. Box 62, Station A, 830 Washington St., Boston, Mass." One photograph affixed to each page with caption in gold ink below. Photographs of Arlington Hospital exteriors and interior spaces, nurses, patients in wards, and operating room during surgical procedure.

did.unittitle: Stusick, Stanley S., Springfield Hospital physician album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7.5 x 11.5 inches; 171 items: gelatin silver prints, newspaper clippings, and ephemera. Paper cover, 48 black paper leaves, secured by string. Personal album of physician Stanley S. Stusick, covers a wide variety of activities, including surgical scenes and medical staff at Springfield Hospital, nurses at a Providence, Rhode Island maternity ward, and personal vacations. Many photographs in the album are captioned with dates and locations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 5.75 x 9 inches; 21 photographic prints. Brown leather cover; photographs are adhered to paper or fabric leaves, 1 photograph on front of each leaf; secured with leather string. Sweetland apparatus advertisement, designed by Ernest J. Sweetland, from San Francisco California. Includes images demonstrating the apparatus for drying plaster casts, relieving shock, and warming beds. Some pages have handwritten descriptions on the reverse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 11.5 x 10.75. 4 photographic prints, 10.25 x 8 inches. Advertising materials from the Syfogen Company, Inc., 1123 Broadway, New York, N. Y., for naso-therapy unit and related supplies. Materials include photographs of equipent and of female models using equipment; price lists, order forms, advertising fliers, and instructions for use.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 7 x 9.25 inches, unbound; 17 gelatin silver prints. 16 photographic prints of page layouts with portraits, 1 non-composite image. Loose pages from a yearbook-like album for a Ukranian medical school. Each page contains portraits of professors and students, as well as one "scene" from school. Also includes one full page portrait. Students are male and female. Some of the pages have holes where photographs were cut out.

did.unittitle: U. S. Army Allerey Hospital Center, World War I, France

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic prints removed from an album; 64 photographic prints, multiple sizes, from 2.25 x 3.25 inches to 4.75 x 6.75 inches. Notable images include the camp hospital, Red Cross stations, sterilizing rooms, and holiday meals with the staff.

did.unittitle: U.S. Army Base Hospital, Camp Mills, Mineola, New York, World War I

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 5.5 x 8 inches; 25 photographic prints, 2 x 3.5 inches and 5 x 7 inches. Unbound black paper album leaves, photographs adhered to pages. Includes photographs of nurses and doctors, mess hall, camp, kitchen, and hospital of Camp Mills military base in Mineola, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album dimensions: 13 x 14.5 inches; 12 photographic prints, 7 x 9 inches. Title on cover: "Université de Bordeaux." Leaves are off white board, photographs are mounted and matted, with captions below. Reverse of each photograph stamped "Université de Bordeaux, faculté de Médecine & de Pharmacie." Photographs show building exteriors and interiors, including dissection hall, laboratory, and museum.

did.unittitle: Walter Reed Maxillo-Facial Surgery Department, World War I soldier case files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 107 photographic prints, multiple sizes. Photographer: Alice L. Becht. Photographs of World War I soldiers who were treated by the Maxillo-Facial Department of Walter Reed United States Army Hospital. Images include the patients before and after reconstructive surgery and the facial and dental implants that were used. Reverse of many photographs includes patient name written in pencil and stamp:"Maxillo-facial Dep't, Walter Reed Gen. Hospital, U.S. Army, Photo A. L. Becht."

odd.head: General
odd.p: This series is unprocessed. Contact the Medical Historical Library at historical.library@yale.edu to request materials that can not be requested using Archives at Yale.
